steps.title: Use the given ratio
steps.content:
The problem gives $BC=EF=28$ and
$$\frac{BC}{BA}=\frac{EF}{ED}$$
Substitute the equal side lengths.
$$\frac{28}{BA}=\frac{28}{ED}$$
So $BA=ED$.

steps.title: Apply SAS
steps.content: The triangles already have two pairs of equal sides, $BA=ED$ and $BC=EF$, and the included angles at $B$ and $E$ are equal because each measures $40^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Conclude no more is needed
steps.content: Those facts prove the triangles congruent by side-angle-side using only the information given in the question. The answer is D.
